name: Kouloura Rolls & Buns
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons Sugar (OR 5 tablespoons Sugar if making)
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve yeast in warm water. Set aside.
2. Bring milk to a boil. Add sugar, butter, and salt. Allow mixture to cool slightly.
3. In a large bowl, combine about 6 cups of flour with a tablespoon left in the measuring cup for your hands. Make a hole in the center of the flour. Pour the liquid into the hole. Add yeast and egg.
4. Mix with a spoon or paddle, pushing flour from all sides into the center. The dough will be sticky until the flour is fully absorbed. Begin kneading the dough in the bowl, then transfer to a lightly floured surface. Continue kneading until the dough is smooth and elastic, about 20 minutes.
5. Place the dough in a greased bowl. Cover with a damp towel. Set aside in a warm place to rise until doubled in size, about 4 hours.
6. Punch down the dough in the middle. Knead for a few minutes and shape into a round loaf or individual rolls (see below). Using fingers, rub the top with egg yolk and sprinkle with sesame seeds. Place in greased baking pans. Let rise once more, uncovered, in a warm place, approximately 1 hour.
7. Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F for 45 minutes. Remove from oven; allow to cool 10 minutes before removing bread from the pan.
To Shape Traditional Kouloura:
- Divide dough into three long ropes. Braid together. Place braid in a greased tube pan or large round baking pan. Stretch dough so ends meet. Use egg yolk to stick ends together.
To Shape Koulourakia Rolls:
- Pinch off pieces of dough. Roll on the board with the palms of your hands to make ropes about 6 inches long. Twist into a hairpin coil, coiling the ends around each other and pressing tips together with a dab of egg yolk. Alternatively, coil like a snake or roll into small round balls using the palms of your hands. Do not flatten as you roll.
